events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a pressure testing situation where the field photo is paired with a cropped sheet excerpt, but the title block, revision, and matching callout are not visible. This means the installed condition cannot be tied to the controlling page. The reference material is ASME B31.3 §345.

The key issue here is that without being able to tie the installed condition to the controlling document (the drawing/specification), we cannot verify compliance. This is a documentation/traceability issue.

Since the work is at the "tested/inspected" stage but we cannot verify it against the controlling document, the appropriate decision would be "needs_more_info" - we need the full drawing with title block, revision, and callout to verify the installed condition matches the controlling page.

Wait, but the instructions say "If the work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail." This isn't dangerous or non-compliant per se - it's that we can't verify it. So "needs_more_info" seems appropriate.

Let me think about this more carefully. The scenario says the installed condition cannot be tied to the controlling page because the title block, revision, and matching callout are not visible. This is a documentation deficiency. We can't confirm the work is compliant, but we also can't confirm it's non-compliant. 

The appropriate response is "needs_more_info" with a medium risk (since we can't verify the pressure test was done correctly against the right specification).

Let me construct the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "needs_more_info",

  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",

  "findings": [
    "Field photo paired with a cropped sheet excerpt that omits title block, revision, and matching callout — installed condition cannot be tied to the controlling document page",
    "Without traceability to the controlling drawing revision and callout, pressure-test acceptance criteria (test pressure, medium, duration) per ASME B31.3 §345 cannot be verified against the specified requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Obtain the full, unredacted drawing sheet showing title block, revision number/date, and the callout that corresponds to the pressure-tested piping",
    "Confirm the test pressure, test medium, hold duration, and acceptance criteria from the controlling document match what was performed and recorded in the test report",
    "Re-submit documentation package with complete drawing references for third-party inspector review before sign-off"
  ],
  "rationale": "The pressure test has been performed and inspected, but the cropped excerpt lacks the title block, revision, and callout needed to tie the installed condition to the controlling document. ASME B31.3 §345 requires that test pressures and procedures conform to specified criteria; without document traceability, compliance cannot be confirmed or denied. This is a documentation gap requiring additional information rather than a confirmed non-compliance.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B31.3 §345 — Pressure Testing"
  ]
}
